User task:

Theorem title: Weak solution equals unique minimizer of the Dirichlet energy

Ambient theorem statement:
Weak solution equals unique minimizer of the Dirichlet energy.

Let \(\Omega \subset \mathbb{R}^d\) be a bounded domain with sufficiently regular boundary, and let \(f \in L^2(\Omega)\). Define
\[
J(v) := \frac12 \int_\Omega |\nabla v|^2 \, dx - \int_\Omega f v \, dx
\qquad \text{for } v \in H_0^1(\Omega).
\]

Then \(u \in H_0^1(\Omega)\) satisfies
\[
\int_\Omega \nabla u \cdot \nabla \varphi \, dx
=
\int_\Omega f \varphi \, dx
\qquad \text{for every } \varphi \in H_0^1(\Omega)
\]
if and only if \(u\) is the unique minimizer of \(J\) over \(H_0^1(\Omega)\).

Target node:

{
  "id": "n1",
  "title": "Energy gap identity from the weak equation",
  "informal_statement": "If \\(u\\in H_0^1(\\Omega)\\) satisfies the weak equation, then for every \\(v\\in H_0^1(\\Omega)\\) one has\n\\[\nJ(v)=J(u)+\\frac12\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla(v-u)|^2\\,dx.\n\\]\nIn particular, \\(J(v)\\ge J(u)\\), with equality only when \\(v=u\\) in \\(H_0^1(\\Omega)\\).",
  "informal_proof_text": "Write \\(v=u+w\\) with \\(w\\in H_0^1(\\Omega)\\), expand \\(|\\nabla(u+w)|^2\\), and regroup terms as \\(J(u)\\) plus a linear bracket plus \\(\\frac12\\int |\\nabla w|^2\\). The weak formulation with test function \\(\\varphi=w\\) kills the linear bracket, leaving the exact gap identity. Nonnegativity of the last term gives minimality, and vanishing of the gap forces \\(\\nabla(v-u)=0\\), hence \\(v=u\\) in \\(H_0^1(\\Omega)\\).",
  "formalization_priority": 1,
  "formalization_rationale": "Best formal island: the exact energy decomposition is compact, reusable, and directly yields both minimality and uniqueness from the weak equation."
}
